summarization.a: The figure displays X-ray Absorption Spectroscopy (XAS) data for O 1s transitions in TFY samples, comparing 'as deposited' and 'forming gas annealed' conditions at various temperatures.
summarization.b: The figure displays X-ray Absorption Spectroscopy (XAS) data for Fe 2p transitions in TFY samples, comparing 'as deposited' and 'forming gas annealed' conditions at various temperatures.

a.question: Does the doping of iron oxide have a big effect on the bindingstates of the iron oxide?
a.answer: a	All peaks at 375 degrees Celsius have similar shape, indicating that doping does not have a huge effect.

a.question: What XAS spectrum could be best added to this set of XAS images to get the total view of the film?
a.answer: To have a good overview the spectrum of magnesium could be added to get a better understanding of why shifts are present in the given spectra.

b.question: For which deposition temperature does the FGA step most influence the film?
b.answer: b	At 375 degrees celcius the bold and normal lines doe not overlap, indicating that FGA has the most effect on films grown at lower temperatures.

b.question: What photon energy do the iron atoms absorb the most?
b.answer: b	Photons with an energy around 710 eV.
